skill.name: codebase-cleanup-deps-audit
skill.description: Dependency security expert for vulnerability scanning, license compliance, and supply chain security analysis.
skill.summary: Analyzes project dependencies for known vulnerabilities, licensing issues, outdated packages, and provides actionable remediation strategies.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis detected 50 patterns in 2 files (821 lines), but all findings are false positives from markdown documentation containing code examples. The skill is a legitimate dependency audit tool with no malicious intent. Minor risk indicators exist due to documented use of external commands and network APIs for dependency scanning.

medium_findings.title: Static Analysis False Positives - External Commands
medium_findings.description: Static scanner detected 33 'backtick execution' and shell command patterns. These are all false positives - the patterns exist in markdown code blocks (```python, ```bash, ```yaml) within documentation files, not in executable code. The skill references external commands like npm audit, pip, and git for legitimate dependency scanning operations.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: Confirmed files are markdown documentation with fenced code blocks - patterns are examples, not executable code.

low_findings.title: Network API References in Documentation
low_findings.description: Static scanner detected fetch calls and HTTP client usage. These are documentation examples showing how to call vulnerability databases (npm audit API, PyPI, OSS Index) for legitimate security scanning purposes.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: URLs point to legitimate security APIs (registry.npmjs.org, pypi.org, bundlephobia.com) used for dependency vulnerability scanning.

low_findings.title: Filesystem Operations in Examples
low_findings.description: Hard link creation pattern detected in Python code example for dependency tree analysis. This is documentation showing file operations for scanning project directories.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Pattern found in code example for dependency discovery - uses pathlib for legitimate file scanning.

content.value_statement: Keep your projects secure by identifying vulnerable, outdated, or license-incompatible dependencies. This skill scans your dependency tree, checks vulnerability databases, and provides prioritized remediation steps.
actual_capabilities: Inventory direct and transitive dependencies across multiple package managers (npm, pip, gem, maven, go, cargo)
Scan dependencies against vulnerability databases (npm audit, PyPI safety, OSS Index)
Analyze license compatibility and identify copyleft or proprietary license conflicts
Identify outdated packages with prioritized upgrade recommendations
Check for supply chain risks including typosquatting and maintainer changes
Generate automated remediation scripts and pull request templates
limitations: Requires access to dependency manifest files (package.json, requirements.txt, etc.)
Cannot automatically update dependencies without user confirmation
Vulnerability data depends on external API availability
Does not test actual runtime behavior of dependencies

use_cases.title: Pre-Release Security Audit
use_cases.description: Run a comprehensive dependency audit before releasing a new version to identify and fix vulnerabilities that could affect users.

use_cases.title: License Compliance Review
use_cases.description: Verify that all dependencies have compatible licenses before integrating into a commercial product to avoid legal risks.

use_cases.title: Technical Debt Assessment
use_cases.description: Identify outdated dependencies and prioritize updates based on age, breaking changes, and security impact.

prompt_templates.title: Quick Vulnerability Scan
prompt_templates.prompt: Scan my project for dependency vulnerabilities. Check package.json and report any critical or high severity issues with recommended fixes.
prompt_templates.scenario: You need a fast security check before deploying.

prompt_templates.title: Full Dependency Audit
prompt_templates.prompt: Perform a complete dependency audit including vulnerability scanning, license compliance check, and outdated package analysis. Prioritize findings by severity and provide actionable remediation steps.
prompt_templates.scenario: Preparing for a security review or compliance audit.

prompt_templates.title: License Compatibility Check
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze all dependencies for license compatibility with our MIT-licensed project. Flag any GPL, AGPL, or proprietary licenses and suggest alternatives.
prompt_templates.scenario: Ensuring legal compliance before open-sourcing a project.

prompt_templates.title: Automated Update Plan
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a prioritized dependency update plan. Group updates by risk level (security patches first, then major versions), estimate effort for each, and generate update commands.

output_examples.input: Scan for vulnerabilities in a Node.js project with 45 dependencies
output: Critical: lodash <4.17.21 - Prototype Pollution (CVE-2021-23337) - Upgrade to 4.17.21
High: minimist <1.2.6 - Prototype Pollution (CVE-2021-44906) - Upgrade to 1.2.6
High: node-fetch <2.6.7 - Information Disclosure (CVE-2022-0235) - Upgrade to 2.6.7
Recommended Action: Run npm audit fix --force to apply patches

output_examples.input: Check license compliance for a commercial project
output: GPL-3.0: package-name - Copyleft license incompatible with proprietary use
Unknown: legacy-lib - License not specified, legal review required
Recommendation: Replace GPL dependency with MIT alternative or obtain commercial license

best_practices: Run dependency audits on a regular schedule (weekly or before each release)
Pin dependency versions in lock files to ensure reproducible builds
Review and test security updates in staging before deploying to production
anti_patterns: Ignoring critical vulnerabilities because tests pass locally
Updating all dependencies at once without testing each change
Using dependencies with unknown or incompatible licenses in commercial products

faq.question: What package managers does this skill support?
faq.answer: The skill supports npm/Yarn (JavaScript), pip/Poetry (Python), gem (Ruby), maven/gradle (Java), go modules, cargo (Rust), composer (PHP), and NuGet (.NET).

faq.question: How does the skill check for vulnerabilities?
faq.answer: It queries public vulnerability databases including npm audit API, PyPI safety database, GitHub Security Advisories, and Sonatype OSS Index to match your dependencies against known CVEs.

faq.question: Can this skill automatically fix vulnerabilities?
faq.answer: The skill generates remediation commands and pull request templates, but requires user confirmation before making any changes to your project.

faq.question: What should I do if a dependency has no license?
faq.answer: Treat unlicensed dependencies as high risk. Contact the maintainer to clarify licensing, or replace with a properly licensed alternative to avoid legal exposure.

faq.question: How often should I run dependency audits?
faq.answer: Run audits weekly via CI/CD, before each release, and immediately when new vulnerabilities are disclosed in your dependency tree.

faq.question: Does this skill check transitive dependencies?
faq.answer: Yes, the skill analyzes both direct dependencies and transitive dependencies (dependencies of dependencies) to provide complete coverage.
